raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, gap, and contribution framing at the level of the overall research claim.","R1.2":"A general draft-improvement request entails checking whether the argument from method to results to significance is coherent.","R1.3":"The draft makes quantitative and ablation-based claims, so improvement includes ensuring those claims match the reported evidence.","R1.5":"The artifact is a compact research draft whose narrative sequence and emphasis may need section-level or paragraph-block reorganization.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the direct request to improve a draft strongly implies sentence-level clarity, flow, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection of the task must preserve uncertainty about claims not verifiable from the artifact alone.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong first-work and future-dominance claims, making overclaim avoidance an always-active responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft improvement rather than executing unrelated research, editing, or evaluation tasks beyond the delegation.","RX.4":"The projection must account for avoiding downstream scholarly harm from inflated, misleading, or unsupported research claims.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the supplied draft and distinguish artifact-supported responsibilities from external assumptions."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, gap, and contribution framing at the level of the overall research claim.
rationale.R1.2: A general draft-improvement request entails checking whether the argument from method to results to significance is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes quantitative and ablation-based claims, so improvement includes ensuring those claims match the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: The artifact is a compact research draft whose narrative sequence and emphasis may need section-level or paragraph-block reorganization.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the direct request to improve a draft strongly implies sentence-level clarity, flow, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of the task must preserve uncertainty about claims not verifiable from the artifact alone.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong first-work and future-dominance claims, making overclaim avoidance an always-active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft improvement rather than executing unrelated research, editing, or evaluation tasks beyond the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must account for avoiding downstream scholarly harm from inflated, misleading, or unsupported research claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the supplied draft and distinguish artifact-supported responsibilities from external assumptions.
